appeal_letter:
Dear UnitedHealthcare Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of the prior authorization request to transfer the patient from acute hospital care to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (IRF). The denial states that, under UnitedHealthcare guidelines, admission to an IRF requires evidence that the patient can actively participate in and benefit from at least three hours of intensive therapy per day, that the clinical notes from the hospital physical therapist indicated the patient fatigued after 30 minutes of therapy on the prior day, and that the plan is therefore authorizing a transfer to a Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) instead.

I respectfully ask the plan to reconsider this determination in light of more current information about the patient's therapy tolerance. The patient suffered a severe stroke and is highly motivated to recover. According to the clinical context, the single day on which the patient fatigued after 30 minutes was the day immediately following the stroke. Since then, the patient has been completing approximately two hours of therapy per day, and the assessment relied upon in the denial reflects older notes rather than the patient's current tolerance.

The basis of this appeal is that the denial appears to rest on a single early data point that no longer reflects the patient's demonstrated ability to participate in therapy, and that a Skilled Nursing Facility would not provide the intensive daily therapy the patient needs to work toward regaining the ability to walk. I ask the plan to consider the patient's current therapy participation in evaluating whether the IRF criteria are met.

I request that UnitedHealthcare overturn the denial and approve the transfer to an Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility. In the alternative, I request a written explanation of the specific documentation the plan requires to demonstrate the ability to participate in at least three hours of intensive therapy per day, together with the opportunity to submit updated therapy records.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Thank you for your reconsideration of this matter.

Sincerely,
The Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Updated physical therapy notes documenting the patient's current daily therapy tolerance of approximately two hours
Treating physician's or physiatrist's statement supporting the patient's ability to participate in and benefit from intensive therapy
Dated therapy logs showing the progression from the immediate post-stroke day to current participation levels
Hospital records documenting the severity and timing of the stroke
Functional status assessments relevant to rehabilitation potential and the goal of regaining walking ability
Clarification of which therapy notes the plan reviewed and their dates relative to the stroke
